name: Grok 4.20
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (size undisclosed); single-model reasoning configuration with native tool use, sibling to the 4-agent 'Grok 4.20 Multi-Agent' variant
note: xAI's March 2026 flagship reasoning model, sitting chronologically between Grok 4 Heavy (Jul 2025) and Grok 4.3 (Apr 2026). Public beta launched Feb 17, 2026 as 'Grok 4.20 (Beta)'; full Enterprise API GA on Mar 10, 2026. Official model slug (grok-4.20-0309-reasoning) and every documented alias use '4.20' — press/resellers sometimes informally shorten it to 'Grok 4.2', but xAI's own docs never use that spelling.

results.notes: Directly fetched Artificial Analysis's own IFBench leaderboard page, which explicitly states: 'Grok 4.3 (medium) scores the highest on IFBench with a score of 83.3%, followed by Grok 4.20 0309 (Reasoning) with a score of 82.9%, and MiniMax-M3 with a score of 82.9%.' This unambiguously names the base/single-agent 'Grok 4.20 0309 (Reasoning)' model (matching this corpus entry, not the Multi-Agent sibling).

Arena.ai (formerly LMArena) Text Leaderboard, human-preference Elo. Row explicitly reads "grok-4.20-beta-0309-reasoning" / SpaceXAI, rank 21, score 1474±4 — this matches this corpus entry's specific model slug (grok-4.20-0309-reasoning, the single-agent Reasoning variant), not the separate 'grok-4.20-beta1' row (rank 19, 1474±5, likely the sibling non-reasoning/base beta) or the Multi-Agent variant. Confirmed by fetching the live leaderboard table twice (once via targeted search, once via a full rank 15-25 table dump) with consistent results. This fills a genuine gap: the existing corpus entry had only one tracked result (IFBench 82.9 from Artificial Analysis).

verificationNotes: CONFIRMED REAL, not a hoax/joke despite the meme-adjacent number. Verified directly via primary source docs.x.ai/developers/release-notes (fetched live): under 'March' it states 'Mar 10 — Grok 4.20 and Grok 4.20 Multi-agent are live.' I also fetched docs.x.ai/developers/models/grok-4.20-reasoning directly: it is a live, current model card for slug `grok-4.20-0309-reasoning` (aliases: grok-4.20, grok-4.20-reasoning, grok-4.20-beta, grok-4.20-0309, grok-4.20-experimental-beta-0304, grok-4.20-reasoning-gv2, etc.), confirming context window 1,000,000 tokens, modalities text+image->text, capabilities (function calling, structured outputs, reasoning), and pricing $1.25/1M input ($0.20/1M cached) / $2.50/1M output. docs.x.ai/developers/models (main index, 'last updated July 9, 2026') independently references 'models grok-4.20 and newer' in a footnote, confirming the family is still recognized as of today. Independent corroboration: OpenRouter lists a 'Grok 4.20 - API Pricing & Benchmarks' page (openrouter.ai/x-ai/grok-4.20); Artificial Analysis profiles it at artificialanalysis.ai/models/grok-4-20 (Intelligence Index 37, 'above average', now flagged as superseded by Grok 4.3 (high) in their own banner -- confirming it, not disproving it); LLM Stats independently tracks 'Grok-4.20 Beta Reasoning' / 'Grok-4.20 Multi-Agent Beta' (released Mar 9, 2026); cloudprice.net's independently compiled version table lists 'Grok 4.20 | 2026-03-31 | Available'. Press/blog coverage (natural20.com citing NextBigFuture and Yahoo Finance, aimlapi.com, Grokipedia, two Japanese SaaS changelogs at tenbin.ai and knowleful.ai) independently corroborates the Feb 17, 2026 public-beta date graduating to the Mar 10, 2026 API GA I used for releaseDate. UNRESOLVED DISCREPANCY flagged rather than guessed: docs.x.ai's own model card states context window as 1,000,000 tokens (verified directly, appears identically on the reasoning and multi-agent pages), but several independent secondary aggregators (Artificial Analysis, cloudprice.net, LLM Stats, benchable.ai) uniformly report 2,000,000 instead -- possibly a later 'v2' point-release (Artificial Analysis's page is literally titled 'Grok 4.20 0309 v2 (Reasoning)... Released April 2026', and docs.x.ai does list a 'grok-4.20-reasoning-gv2' alias consistent with a v2 revision) rather than the original March 10 launch config. I trusted the directly-fetched, still-live primary source (ctxK=1000) over aggregator consensus and note the conflict here rather than silently picking one. Pricing corroboration: $1.25/$2.50/$0.20-cached matches exactly between docs.x.ai (primary) and benchable.ai (independent OpenRouter-style listing, model id x-ai/grok-4.20-20260309); Artificial Analysis's differing $2.00/$6.00 figure is attributed to that same later 'v2' revision, not the launch config, so I did not use it. NO VERIFIED BENCHMARK SCORES: xAI never published an official Grok 4.20 blog post or benchmark table -- natural20.com explicitly notes 'xAI has still not published an official blog post about 4.20. The last entry on x.ai/news remains the Grok 4.1 announcement,' and x.ai/news/grok-4-20 404s. LLM Stats independently shows 'Score pending' for every Grok 4.20 variant, corroborating the absence of trustworthy benchmark data. Numbers circulating on lower-tier SEO blogs (tokenmix.ai: 78% SWE-bench/91.2% MMLU/1385 Elo; hokai.io: 88.9% GPQA Diamond, which suspiciously matches the older Grok 4 Heavy GPQA figure already in this corpus, suggesting a copy error) are single-sourced and uncorroborated, so per instructions I omitted all of them rather than guess. Artificial Analysis's own composite Intelligence Index (37) has no matching slug in the approved benchmark-slug enum, so -- following the same precedent set by the existing grok-4-3-high.json entry -- I left results empty rather than force a mismatched slug. paramsB left null per corpus convention: Artificial Analysis's own FAQ for this exact model states 'SpaceXAI has not disclosed the model size or parameter count'; one secondary blog (natural20.com) quotes an alleged Musk X post claiming 'This is just our V8 small foundation model, so 500B params' for the initial beta, but this is unconfirmed by any primary source so I did not use it. Family name reasoning: existing per-point-release Grok families in this corpus each get their own family string matching the release number (Grok 4 -> family 'Grok 4'; Grok 4.3 / Grok 4.3 (High) -> family 'Grok 4.3'). Grok 4.20 is its own numbered generation marker between Grok 4(-Heavy) and Grok 4.3, so I used family 'Grok 4.20' to match that convention.
